skill.name: shadcn-ui-patterns
skill.description: Guides agents to build React UI with ShadCN patterns, Radix accessibility primitives, TailwindCSS utilities, and tested component conventions.
skill.summary: Build accessible ShadCN UI components with consistent forms, dialogs, variants, TailwindCSS, and testing patterns.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical signals, but review found they are caused by Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, component names, and documentation links. The only confirmed concern is low risk guidance to run unpinned npx shadcn@latest commands, which is legitimate for this skill but should be reviewed before execution.

low_findings.title: Unpinned npx Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill recommends npx shadcn@latest add commands for installing UI components. This is normal ShadCN usage, but it downloads current npm package content at runtime and can change over time.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is present in a bash example and would execute external package code if a user runs it. The surrounding text shows legitimate component installation guidance, not malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill lists official ShadCN and Radix documentation URLs. These are user-facing references and not automated network requests, so the static network findings are low risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a Resources section and no code sends data to them. They point to expected documentation sites for this UI guidance skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Most external command alerts come from fenced Markdown code blocks, inline component names, props, and TypeScript examples. No Ruby or shell backtick execution logic is present in the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are Markdown examples for React, ShadCN components, TailwindCSS, and tests. They are not executable skill code and the allowed tools are limited to reading and searching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Signals Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary UI text such as the ShadCN name, destructive button variants, DialogDescription components, toast descriptions, and test names. No cryptographic implementation is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain UI vocabulary and component identifiers, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or other crypto code. The file is an instruction document, not a cryptographic module.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Key File Signal Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The certificate or key finding maps to a keyboard test example using the Escape key. It does not reference certificate files, private keys, tokens, or credential material.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 402 is a UI test interaction with the Escape key. There is no file path, credential variable, or secret value on that line.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance Signals Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to form validation and explanatory HTML rendering text. No host probing, shell enumeration, environment discovery, or system inventory command is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are an email validation schema and a note about rendered nested buttons. They do not contain reconnaissance commands or system inspection logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical heuristic combined unrelated Markdown examples, documentation URLs, and a keyboard test string. Review found no credential access, data exfiltration, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious control flow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The only real external command is documented ShadCN installation guidance, and the network evidence is a Resources list. No semantic evidence supports the scanner heuristic.

content.value_statement: Teams often create inconsistent React components that miss accessibility and design-system rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward ShadCN, Radix UI, TailwindCSS, and testing patterns.
actual_capabilities: Recommends ShadCN component installation commands for common UI primitives.
Shows preferred usage for buttons, dialogs, forms, sheets, popovers, and tabs.
Guides accessible dialog structure with titles, descriptions, triggers, and focus behavior.
Encourages React Hook Form and Zod validation for form implementations.
Promotes TailwindCSS utilities, dark mode classes, and the cn helper for conditional styles.
Includes testing examples for dialog open, close, and keyboard interaction behavior.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include executable helper scripts.
Version guidance mentions November 2025 and may require review as ShadCN changes.
It focuses on React, Next.js, TailwindCSS, Radix UI, and ShadCN workflows.
Installation commands use the latest package tag, so teams may prefer pinned versions.

use_cases.title: Create Accessible Product UI
use_cases.description: Build dialogs, forms, buttons, and panels with consistent ShadCN component structure and Radix accessibility behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Component Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Check whether UI changes use ShadCN variants, form validation, dialog titles, and TailwindCSS conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design System Usage
use_cases.description: Align application teams around common component choices, dark mode patterns, and reusable UI primitives.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Basic Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one UI component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ShadCN-based React component for [feature]. Use existing UI primitives, semantic button variants, and TailwindCSS utilities.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Dialog Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dialog implementation for ShadCN and Radix accessibility patterns. Check title structure, trigger usage, focus behavior, and keyboard support.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Validated Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a form for [workflow] using ShadCN Form, React Hook Form, Zod validation, FormMessage, and accessible labels.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a UI Surface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a larger interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React UI surface against ShadCN patterns. Identify component replacements, variant issues, accessibility gaps, testing needs, and TailwindCSS cleanup.

output_examples.input: Build a delete confirmation dialog for a project settings page.
output_examples.output: A review-ready dialog plan with a destructive action button, clear title, explanatory description, accessible trigger, and cancel path.

output_examples.input: Review a sign-in form that uses plain inputs and no validation messages.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist that replaces plain fields with ShadCN Form components, adds Zod validation, and surfaces field-level errors.
A component usage recommendation with Button variants, icon button sizing, dark mode utility classes, and testing notes.

best_practices: Use ShadCN primitives before creating custom components for common UI interactions.
Keep accessibility structure visible in reviews, especially dialog titles, descriptions, triggers, and form labels.
Review installation commands and package versions before running them in production projects.
anti_patterns: Do not replace ShadCN buttons with custom styled native buttons without a clear reason.
Do not omit DialogTitle, FormLabel, or FormMessage when they are needed for accessible UI.
Do not hardcode colors or inline styles when variants and TailwindCSS utilities fit the need.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React UI components with ShadCN, Radix UI accessibility patterns, TailwindCSS utilities, and common testing guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill file is instructional and its allowed tools are limited to reading and searching files.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked low?
faq.answer: The scanner found many false positives in Markdown examples. The only confirmed concern is unpinned npx installation guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights dialog structure, trigger behavior, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, labels, and validation messages.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: It targets React and Next.js style projects that use ShadCN UI, Radix UI, TailwindCSS, React Hook Form, and Zod.

faq.question: Should teams pin ShadCN package versions?
faq.answer: Teams with strict release controls should review or pin package versions before running npx commands from the guidance.
